revision.text:
This {{{1|article or section}}} relies largely or entirely upon a '''single [[{{SITENAME}}:Citing sources|source]].'''
You can [[{{SITENAME}}:WikiProject Fact and Reference Check|help]] Wikipedia by introducing appropriate citations to additional sources.
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|This article has been tagged since '''{{{date}}}'''.}}
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|[[Category:Articles lacking sources from {{{date}}}]]|[[Category:Articles lacking sources]]}}[[Category:All articles lacking sources]] This text and the following text does not appear in the template. Alternative templates are at [[Template:Unreferenced]] and [[Template:Unreferenced-section]]. [[Category:Wikipedia maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

This {{{1|article or section}}} relies largely or entirely upon a '''single [[{{SITENAME}}:Citing sources|source]].'''
You can [[{{SITENAME}}:WikiProject Fact and Reference Check|help]] Wikipedia by introducing appropriate citations to additional sources.
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|This article has been tagged since '''{{{date}}}'''.}}
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|[[Category:Articles lacking sources from {{{date}}}]]|[[Category:Articles lacking sources]]}}[[Category:All articles lacking sources]] ---- * This template will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles lacking sources]]. * This [[{{SITENAME}}:Template|template]] is a [[{{SITENAME}}:Avoid self-references|self-reference]], and thus is not appropriate for [[{{SITENAME}}:Subst]]. * There is currently no consensus about where on the article to place this template. ==Usage== This template also has two optional fields. The first permits the user to specify what needs additional sources. For instance, if the entire article relies on only one source, a user would enter {{onesource|article}}; but should the user wish to be more specific on which section of the article relies only on one source, then the user could enter {{onesource|section called "Childhood"}}. Leaving the parameter undefined is acceptable, as it will print out the default text "article or section". The second field is a date parameter, entered as "date=''month year''". Adding this new (as of November 2006) parameter sorts the article into subcategories of [[:Category:Articles lacking sources by month]] and out of the parent category, allowing the oldest problems to be identified and dealt with first. If the date parameter is omitted, a [[WP:BOT|bot]] will add it later. Note that order does not matter with respect to the above optional parameters. Both {{onesource|section|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} and {{onesource|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|section}} will produce the same result. ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Sources of articles]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] [[Category:{{SITENAME}} maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Citation templates|Citations missing]] [[Category:Cleanup templates|Citations missing]]

This {{{1|article or section}}} relies largely or entirely upon a '''single [[{{SITENAME}}:Citing sources|source]].'''
You can [[{{SITENAME}}:WikiProject Fact and Reference Check|help]] Wikipedia by introducing appropriate citations to additional sources.
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|This article has been tagged since '''{{{date}}}'''.}}
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|[[Category:Articles lacking sources from {{{date}}}]]|[[Category:Articles lacking sources]]}}[[Category:All articles lacking sources]] ---- * This template will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles lacking sources]]. * This [[{{SITENAME}}:Template|template]] is a [[{{SITENAME}}:Avoid self-references|self-reference]], and thus is not appropriate for [[{{SITENAME}}:Subst]]. * There is currently no consensus concerning where on the article to place this template. ==Usage== This template also has two optional fields. The first permits the user to specify what needs additional sources. For instance, if the entire article relies on only one source, a user would enter {{onesource|article}}; but should the user wish to be more specific on which section of the article relies only on one source, then the user could enter {{onesource|section called "Childhood"}}. Leaving the parameter undefined is acceptable, as it will print out the default text "article or section". The second field is a date parameter, entered as "date=''month year''". Adding this new (as of November 2006) parameter sorts the article into subcategories of [[:Category:Articles lacking sources by month]] and out of the parent category, allowing the oldest problems to be identified and dealt with first. If the date parameter is omitted, a [[WP:BOT|bot]] will add it later. Note that order does not matter with respect to the above optional parameters. Both {{onesource|section|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} and {{onesource|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|section}} will produce the same result. ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Sources of articles]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] [[Category:{{SITENAME}} maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Citation templates|Citations missing]] [[Category:Cleanup templates|Citations missing]]

{|style="width:100%;background:none" |width=60px|[[Image:Information_icon.svg|40px]] |'''This {{{1|article or section}}} relies largely or entirely upon a [[WP:CITE|single source]].'''
Please help [{{fullurl:{{FULLPAGENAME}}|action=edit}} improve this article] by introducing appropriate citations to additional sources. {{#if:{{{date|}}}|This article has been tagged since {{{date}}}.}} ([[WP:EDIT|help]]) |}
{{#if:{{{date|}}}|[[Category:Articles lacking sources from {{{date}}}]]|[[Category:Articles lacking sources]]}}[[Category:All articles lacking sources]] ---- Place this template at the bottom of the article or section tagged. The simplest way to add this template to an article is to copy and paste {{tlx|onesource|article|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article. This template will categorise tagged articles into [[:Category:All articles lacking sources]] and either [[:Category:Articles lacking sources]] or a monthly category like [[:Category:Articles lacking sources from {{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}]], if a date is supplied. * This [[WP:TM|template]] is a [[WP:SELF|self-reference]]. * Please do not [[WP:SUB|subst:]] this template. == Usage == This template alerts readers that citations in an article or section may be inappropriate or misinterpreted. Examples include quotations taken out of context and false assertions about a source's facts or conclusions. When using this template the text should have: *Citations in footnote, Harvard referencing, or some other standard format. *Multiple citations (or one key citation) that an editor tried to verify and found that the article passage misstated or misconstrued the original source's content. Please try to improve the article or make a good faith attempt to verify the citations in question before adding this template, and discuss the matter on the talk page. If only one citation is problematic, or there is a desire to tag particular citations, consider using {{tl|failed verification}} instead. Articles that merely lack references or have POV problems should be flagged with some other template. ==Technical Details== This template has two optional fields. The first permits the user to specify what needs to be cited. For instance, if the entire article needs to be cited, a user would enter {{tlx|onesource|article}}; but should the user wish to be more specific on which section of the article needs to be cited, then the user could enter {{tlx|onesource|section called "Childhood"}}. Leaving the parameter undefined is acceptable, as it will print out the default text "article or section." The second field is a date parameter, entered as "date=''month year''". Adding this sorts the article into subcategories of [[:Category:Articles lacking sources by month]] and out of the parent category, allowing the oldest problems to be identified and dealt with first. If the date parameter is omitted, a [[WP:BOT|bot]] will add it later. Note that order does not matter with respect to the above optional parameters. Both {{tlx|onesource|section|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} and {{tlx|onesource|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|section}} will produce the same result. ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Sources of articles]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] [[Category:Citation templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Cleanup templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

Articles that merely lack references or have POV problems should be flagged with some other template. ==Technical Details== This template has two optional fields. The first permits the user to specify what needs to be cited. For instance, if the entire article needs to be cited, a user would enter {{tlx|onesource|article}}; but should the user wish to be more specific on which section of the article needs to be cited, then the user could enter {{tlx|onesource|section called "Childhood"}}. Leaving the parameter undefined is acceptable, as it will print out the default text "article or section." The second field is a date parameter, entered as "date=''month year''". Adding this sorts the article into subcategories of [[:Category:Articles lacking sources by month]] and out of the parent category, allowing the oldest problems to be identified and dealt with first. If the date parameter is omitted, a [[WP:BOT|bot]] will add it later. Note that order does not matter with respect to the above optional parameters. Both {{tlx|onesource|section|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} and {{tlx|onesource|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|section}} will produce the same result. ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Sources of articles]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{#if:{{{date|}}}|[[Category:Articles lacking sources from {{{date}}}]]|[[Category:Articles lacking sources]]}}[[Category:All articles lacking sources]] ---- The simplest way to add this template to an article is to copy and paste {{tlx|onesource|article|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article. This template will categorise tagged articles into [[:Category:All articles lacking sources]] and either [[:Category:Articles lacking sources]] or a monthly category like [[:Category:Articles lacking sources from {{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}]], if a date is supplied. * This [[WP:TM|template]] is a [[WP:SELF|self-reference]]. * Please do not [[WP:SUB|subst:]] this template. * There is currently no consensus as to where on the article to place templates such as this. == Usage == This template alerts readers that citations in an article or section may be inappropriate or misinterpreted. Examples include quotations taken out of context and false assertions about a source's facts or conclusions. When using this template the text should have: *Citations in footnote, Harvard referencing, or some other standard format. *Multiple citations (or one key citation) that an editor tried to verify and found that the article passage misstated or misconstrued the original source's content. Please try to improve the article or make a good faith attempt to verify the citations in question before adding this template, and discuss the matter on the talk page. If only one citation is problematic, or there is a desire to tag particular citations, consider using {{tl|failed verification}} instead. Articles that merely lack references or have POV problems should be flagged with some other template. ==Technical Details== This template has two optional fields. The first permits the user to specify what needs to be cited. For instance, if the entire article needs to be cited, a user would enter {{tlx|onesource|article}}; but should the user wish to be more specific on which section of the article needs to be cited, then the user could enter {{tlx|onesource|section called "Childhood"}}. Leaving the parameter undefined is acceptable, as it will print out the default text "article or section." The second field is a date parameter, entered as "date=''month year''". Adding this sorts the article into subcategories of [[:Category:Articles lacking sources]], allowing the oldest problems to be identified and dealt with first. If the date parameter is omitted, a [[WP:BOT|bot]] will add it later. Note that order does not matter with respect to the above optional parameters. Both {{tlx|onesource|section|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} and {{tlx|onesource|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|section}} will produce the same result. ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Sources of articles]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]
